data.title: CookieYes Launches Cookie Policy Generator as Global Privacy Enforcement Intensifies
data.description: CookieYes, a leading provider of consent management solutions, has introduced a new tool tailored for expanding businesses. This tool streamlines cookie disclosures and ensures that policies are always in sync with up-to-the-minute alterations on websites. The announcement was made on April 1, 2026, in MILTON KEYNES, England.
SAUDI ARABIA REPORTS INTERCEPTING TWO DRONES IN THE PAST FEW HOURS.

data.title: Share buy-back programme – transactions March
data.description: Gabriel Holding A/S started a share repurchase program on March 16, 2026. The program will be active from March 16, 2026, until March 16, 2027. Within this timeframe, the company has the authority to buy back a total of 94,500 shares, representing approximately 5% of the company's share capital.
Total voting rights and capital information disclosed with numbers unmentioned.

data.title: Norsk Hydro: Invitation to Hydro's first quarter results 2026
data.description: The quarterly results for Hydro in the first quarter of 2026 are set to be announced at 07:00 CEST on April 29, 2026. This corresponds to 01:00 EDT, 06:00 BST, and 05:00 UTC/GMT. The report and presentation for this quarter will be accessible on hydro.com simultaneously with the official releases.

data.title: Construction contract (Supporting infrastructure)
data.description: Nordecon AS and its subsidiary Embach Ehitus OÜ have partnered with the Estonian Centre for Defence Investments to secure a 110.4 million euro contract, excluding VAT, for developing supporting infrastructure in Pärnu County. The construction project is expected to be finished by the summer of 2028.

data.title: Pulsar Helium Announces Director Resignation and Exercise of Stock Options
data.description: This announcement contains confidential information and should not be released, published, or distributed in whole or in part in Australia, Japan, or the Republic of South Africa. It should not be transmitted to, distributed to, or sent by any national, resident, or citizen of these countries or any other jurisdiction where such actions may violate local securities laws or regulations.

data.title: Pre-silent newsletter ahead of Q1 2026 results
data.description: Alm. Brand Group's latest newsletter discusses important trends and factors to provide investors, analysts, and other stakeholders with insights into the company's financial performance. This aims to offer a comprehensive understanding of the business's key metrics and strategies to stakeholders.

data.title: CFTC's enforcement director warns prediction market insiders of consequences amid belief that insider trading rules don't apply.
data.description: David Miller, the enforcement director of the CFTC, has debunked the misconception that insider trading is not applicable in prediction markets. He emphasized that this belief, commonly perpetuated in mainstream and social media, is incorrect. Miller's statement serves to clarify that insider trading rules do indeed apply in prediction markets, highlighting the necessity for compliance in such platforms. This announcement comes amidst increasing concerns regarding fraudulent activities in prediction markets.

data.title: Mercado Libre will cease Mercado Coin rewards; launches Meli Dolar (MUSD), a stablecoin introduced in 2024.
data.description: Despite the closure of Mercado Coin, Mercado Libre continues its crypto rewards strategy with the introduction of the Meli Dolar (MUSD) in 2024. The MUSD is a US-dollar stablecoin that is being offered as part of the company's initiatives.

data.title: Bitfarms reports $285M loss due to Bitcoin drop, shifts focus to HPC and AI; shares rise despite setback.
data.description: Bitfarms reported being affected by a drop in Bitcoin prices last year, prompting the company to begin transitioning from Bitcoin mining to focusing on High Performance Computing (HPC) and Artificial Intelligence (AI). The transition has been ongoing for five months.

data.title: Crypto Fear and Greed Index stays at 'extreme fear,' but Bitcoin above $60,000 support hints at optimism.
data.description: The Crypto Fear and Greed Index continues to hover in the 'extreme fear' region, indicating caution among investors. Despite this, Bitcoin has held steady above the $60,000 support level for an extended period, suggesting a potential positive outlook.
